content:
Puzzling. Here are the steps as I understand them. And, since the process relies 100% on the OS file management, all should be there.

https://support.mozilla.org/en-US/kb/moving-thunderbird-data-to-a-new-computer

here is the info in brief steps:

  1. ensure thunderbird is NOT running on old machine
  2. enter %appdata% in search window on task bar and press Enter key
  3. this should open windows file explorer in Appdata\roaming folder
  4. highlight the Thunderbird folder and copy to external media
  5. ensure thunderbird is installed on second computer, started once and NOT running
  6. enter %appdata% in search window on task bar and press Enter key
  7. you should be in Appdata\roaming folder. DELETE the thunderbird folder there
  8. now , paste your copied thunderbird folder there. 
#   start thunderbird and all should be there

BUT, if you still have your old profile, I think we can assist in copying them to new profile. If you post screenshot of the old profile folder that inicates the missing folders, we can offer steps to copy them. thank you

content:
If Thunderbird is still installed on the old computer, look in <Settings | Privacy & Security | Passwords | Saved Passwords>. This may reveal your passwords, although if the authentication method for a particular host is OAuth2, you will see an encoded representation of a token, which is not the same as, and can't be used as a password.

Otherwise, you may have to reset your passwords for each email service.
creator: rednoise

content: You can check what the smtp server password was for the old account in the Thunderbird password manager (Settings->Passwords->Saved passwords). If the password and username are indeed the same, and the SMTP settings are exactly the same (server name, port, connection security, authentication method, username) the only possible cause of problem would be the server certificate. In the old Thunderbid you can got to Settings->Security->Certificates->Manage Certificates and check if you may have added a security exception in the Servers tab
